The Facts About
Kent Hovind

A sourced, fact-based reference for journalists, educators, and anyone encountering Kent "Dr. Dino" Hovind's claims. Every assertion on this site links to court documents, peer-reviewed research, or primary sources.

📋

The Record

Tax fraud conviction, fake PhD, and documented harassment. Court records, not opinions.

🔬

The Arguments

Point-by-point rebuttals of creationist talking points with peer-reviewed sources.

🎙️

In His Own Words

Direct quotes and video timestamps paired with what the science actually says.

Who is Kent Hovind?

Kent Hovind (b. 1953) is an American young-earth creationist and tax protester who runs "Dinosaur Adventure Land" — originally in Pensacola, Florida (seized by the government), now rebuilt in Repton, Alabama. He styles himself "Dr. Dino" based on a mail-order PhD from the unaccredited Patriot Bible University.

In 2006, Hovind and his wife were convicted on 58 federal counts including tax evasion, structuring financial transactions to evade reporting requirements, and other offenses. He served approximately eight years in federal prison before his release in 2015.

Despite his criminal record and lack of legitimate academic credentials, Hovind continues to produce content promoting young-earth creationism and attacking evolutionary biology. This site exists to provide accurate, well-sourced responses to his claims.

Editorial standards: This site cites court documents, peer-reviewed journals, and established scientific institutions. If you find an error, email corrections@dinokent.com. We correct mistakes.

DinoKent.com — A fact-based reference. All claims sourced. Found an error? corrections@dinokent.com
